description: Tracks external data sources staged for ingestion into Threatpedia. Used by the data-ingestion task to perform incremental syncs without reprocessing already-ingested records.

sources.name: CISSM Cyber Events Database
sources.provider: University of Maryland — Center for International and Security Studies (CISSM)
sources.description: Comprehensive database of global cyber events including state-sponsored attacks, criminal campaigns, hacktivism, and espionage operations. Each record includes actor attribution, industry targeting, geopolitical alliance flags, and event classification.

ingestionStrategy.dedup: Match on slug field — each CISSM event has a unique slug
ingestionStrategy.mapping: Map CISSM event_type/event_subtype → Threatpedia incident categories; actor → threat-actor-index.json lookup; country → geolocation enrichment
ingestionStrategy.notes: Awaiting web stack and database — CSV staged for future bulk + incremental ingestion. On each sync, fetch new records from cybereventsdatabase.org API (if available) or re-download CSV and diff against lastIngestedRecord date.
